rulings.Case Title: IRVING ZARETSKY VS. MARK CASEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash Depo Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 6, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANTs MARK CASEY, CARRIE CASEY's Motionn To Quash Deposition Subpoena. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS KNOWN OF THE EXISTENCE OF THIS REPORT FOR MONTHS AND NO GOOD CAUSE HAS BEEN SHOWN AS TO WHY DISCOVERY SHOULD BE ALLOWED THIS CLOSE TO TRIAL. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW LOPEZ VS. EIMING JUNG, LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 6, 2012, Line 11, DEFENDANTs SAM SHULMAN, AGGE, INC. DBA BLACK AND WHITE CHECKER CAB's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment: Denied. The objections to Plaintiff's separate statement of facts nos. 35, 39 and 47 are overruled. The Court notes Fact No. 34 is not disputed. The underlying cited discovery items can be fairly read to show that defendant's taxi cab was operating in excess of the conceded speed limit just before the accident, and making inferences in favor of the party opposing this motion (as I am required to do), I infer a similar speed at the time of the accident. The motion for summary adjudication do not present independent grounds, and accordingly is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES W. GAY VS. CREATIVE ALLIANCE, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILTY et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 6, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT CREATIVE ALLIANCE, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILTY COMPANY'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena. The courts adopts Pro Tem Huibonhoa's recommendation as follows: Denied. The parties agree that the automatic stay of 11 U.S.C. § 362(a)(1) does not apply here. The documents sought are directly relevant to the claims in this case, and the relevance of the documents sought outweighs Mr. Coyle's financial privacy. Unless a protective order is already in effect the parties must meet and confer on a protective order to protect Mr. Coyle's financial privacy interests and should submit a stipulated proposed protective order to the Court for approval. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: JOHN ARCHIE MERRILL, AS TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN VS. COURNALE & CO. (CAL DRE LICENSE NO. 00322534) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Clyde Cournale'S Attendance At Deposition; Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 6, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JOHN ARCHIE MERRILL, AS TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN ARCHIE MERILL AND ANN M. MERRILL JOINT LIVING TRUST, DATED 4/19/95's Motion To Compel Clyde Cournale'S Attendance At Deposition; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL MARCH VS. JOHN WICKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 6, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT JOHN WICKER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Although collision is an inherent risk of skiing, Defendant Wicker has failed to demonstrate that as a matter of law his act of intentionally attempting to cut off Plaintiff did not increase the risk of collision with Plaintiff over and above those risks of collision inherent in the sport of skiing. Cheong v. Antablin, 16 Cal.4th 1063 (1997). See Deposition of March at pp. 12, 26; Wicker deposition at 13; 17 (intent to cut off). There is a triable issue of fact, therefore, as to whether Defendant Wicker's conduct was intentional or so reckless as to defeat the assumption of risk defense. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA M SAGONOWSKY VS. CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Chritine Tour-Sarkissian, Paul Tour-Sarkission, Phil Foster, Abigail Morris And The Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices & Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, Llp From Representing James Cantrell And Cantrell, Harris & Associates, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 6, 2012, Line 6, PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY'S Motion To Disqualify Chritine Tour-Sarkissian, Paul Tour-Sarkission, Phil Foster, Abigail Morris And The Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices & Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, Llp From Representing James Cantrell And Cantrell, Harris & Associates, Inc. Denied. Plaintiff did not have a reasonable expectation that her former counsel would withhold information from the present client. Vapnek, Tuft, et al., Cal. Practice Guide: Prof'l Resp., 4:179. The present client was previously retained as an expert witness who testified at trial in the underlying action, which is the subject of the present litigation. The types of communication from the former case at issue (see discussion at Opposition at 9 et seq.) relate to this case and will in any event be disclosed. Styles v. Mumbert, 164 Cal.App.4th 1163, 1167 (2008) is not of assistance to Plaintiff as that (1) states the general rule, an exception to which is that obtaining here and stated in the first sentence of this ruling, and (2) in contrast to Styles, this is, in fact, a malpractice case. Compare id. at 1168. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is unopposed and is accordingly granted. Plaintiff's supplemental request for judicial notice filed in support of the reply is improper, and is thus denied. Objections to the Morris Declaration are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: CATINA JOHNSON et al VS. HAYES VALLEY LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 6, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained in its entirety with leave to amend. Plaintiffs must plead at least the following: (1) The timely filing of a sufficient government claim (Fowler v. Howell (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 1746, 1752); (2) A statutory basis of liability. Gov. Code § 815(a). Civil Code § 1714 standing alone does not provide a statutory basis for liability of a public entity. Zelig v. County of Los Angeles (2002) 27 Cal.4th 1112, 1132. If Plaintiffs wish to plead statutory liability based on Gov. Code § 835, Plaintiffs must plead the existence of some defect in the property itself and the existence of a causal connection between the defect and Plaintiffs' injury. Zelig, 27 Cal.4th at 1138. Plaintiffs may also plead any other statutory basis for liability that they believe supports the imposition of a legal duty on the housing authority. Id. at pp. 1138-1139. The statutory basis for liability must be pled with particularity, Lopez v. Southern California Rapid Transit District (1980) 40 Cal.3d 780, 795, and (3) Facts sufficient to show that Plaintiffs Terrillynn Cain, Cher'rel Cain, and Alonzo Scott, Jr. have standing under CCP § 377.60. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: CATINA JOHNSON et al VS. HAYES VALLEY LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Mccormack Baron Ragan Management Services, Inc., Hayes Valley Apartments Limited Partnership'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 6, 2012, Line 8, Defendants Mccormack Baron Ragan Management Services, Inc., Hayes Valley Apartments Limited Partnership'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. Sustained with leave to amend on the issue of standing. Plaintiffs must plead facts sufficient to show that Plaintiffs Terrillynn Cain, Cher'rel Cain, and Alonzo Scott, Jr. have standing under CCP § 377.60. On the second cause of action, to the extent that Plaintiffs seek to plead a wrongful death cause of action premised on a breach of contract, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. A wrongful death action must be based on a tortious act. Barrett v. Superior Court (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 1176, 1183. However, a cause of action for wrongful death may be based on a properly pled breach of the warranty of habitability because such actions may sound in tort. Stoiber v. Honeychuck, (1980) 101 Cal.App.3d 903, 917. But the demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained with leave to amend because Plaintiffs have failed to plead sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action for tortious breach of warranty of habitability. Plaintiffs must plead, among other things, facts in support of these elements: (1) there was a materially defective condition affecting habitability; (2) Defendant had actual or constructive knowledge of the defects; (3) the effect of the condition on habitability was not apparent on a reasonable inspection; (4) the Defendant was given notice within a reasonable time after the tenant discovered the breach; and (5) the Defendant was give a reasonable time to correct the defect while the Decedent remained in possession. 12 B. Witkin, SUMMARY OF CALIFORNIA LAW, Real Prop., § 627(10th ed. 2005); Quevado v. Braga (1977) 72 Cal.App.3d 1, 7-8. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 6, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANT ADMIRAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, without leave to amend. The court did not receive courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: JIMMY VALENCIA VS. HSBC BANK USA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 6, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANTS HSBC BANK USA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR, OCWEN LOAN SERVICING, LLC'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Transferred to Department 501 to be heard on August 14, 2012 at 9:30 am. Moving party is to provide courtesy copies of their moving papers and the operative Complaint, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, to Department 501, no later than August 7, 2012 at 4:00 pm. =(302/CK)
